titleStmt.title: Letter from Austin Stack to F.H. O'Donnell, 13 March, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Austin Stack

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Austin Stack, (1879-1929), commandant of the Kerry Bragade of the Irish Volunteers in 1916. F.H.(Frank) O'Donnell , Honorary Secretary of the Kerry County Board of the National Volunteers, 1916.
This letter is an invitation to the Tralee Irish National Volunteers to participate in a parade, along with other organisations and societies, on St. Patrick's Day, March 17th, 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Bureau of Military History Contemporary Documents Frank O'Donnell Collection BMH CD/52/2/21

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
NATIONAL HOLIDAY CELEBRATION TRALEE 13th. March, 1916. F. H. O'Donnell, Esq.,
Hon. Sec.
Irish National Volunteers,
TRALEE Dear Sir, I have been asked to invite your Corps to take part in
Demonstration proposed to be held in honour of Ireland's Patron
Saint on the 17th. inst. The Irish Volunteers and the Irish
Workers' Union, the Trades Council and Tralee Urban Council
have already signified their desire to co-operate, and invitations
are being issued to other local public bodies and societies.
The Demonstration will take the form of a Procession to start
from the '98 Memorial at 1 o'clock and I trust your Corps will
see their way to take part. Yours faithfully, A. Stack
